nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 苏拉威西岛
nodes.summary: 苏拉威西岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联31种鸟类，包括菲律宾冢雉、蓝胸鹑、白喉林鸽等31种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 布顿岛
nodes.summary: 布顿岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括Sulawesi hawk-eagle、蘇拉皺盔犀鳥、Pygmy hanging parrot，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 梅公加山脉
nodes.summary: 梅公加山脉是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Pygmy hanging parrot，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Pygmy hanging parrot
nodes.summary: Pygmy hanging parrot（Pygmy hanging parrot，学名 Loriculus exilis），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联6个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、森林砍伐、猎捕压力。

nodes.name: 印度尼西亚
nodes.summary: 印度尼西亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联297种鸟类，包括细斑树鸭、白腹麻鸭、太平洋黑鴨等297种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 卡巴那島
nodes.summary: 卡巴那島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Pygmy hanging parrot，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 沃沃尼島
nodes.summary: 沃沃尼島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Pygmy hanging parrot，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Loriculus exilis is mainly endemic to Sulawesi island in Indonesia, but can also be found on many small surrounding islands such as Buton, Kabaena, and Wawonii. Although previously thought to occur only in preserved, or primary lowland forest, the species has shown some tolerance to habitat degradation. This said, it inhabits a wide range of forested areas; primary and secondary lowland and hill forests, as well as mangroves and forest edge habitats. It has been said to fly or perch anywhere from sea level up to 1,000 meters, but recent records extend its elevation range to 1,320 m in the Mekongga Mountains.{{Cite journal |last1=Berryman |first1=Alex J

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The population trends for Loriculus exilis are generally unknown, but suspected to be decreasing due to habitat loss from deforestation and trapping for the bird trade. Trapping for the pet trade is a big threat for Indonesian birds, but unfortunately has few field studies to properly gauge how serious that threat is.{{Cite journal |last1=Harris |first1=J

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The population trends for Loriculus exilis are generally unknown, but suspected to be decreasing due to habitat loss from deforestation and trapping for the bird trade. Trapping for the pet trade is a big threat for Indonesian birds, but unfortunately has few field studies to properly gauge how serious that threat is.{{Cite journal |last1=Harris |first1=J

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: |date=April 2017 |title=Measuring the impact of the pet trade on Indonesian birds |url=https://conbio.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/cobi.12729 |journal=Conservation Biology |language=en |volume=31 |issue=2 |pages=394–405 |doi=10.1111/cobi.12729 |pmid=28146342 |bibcode=2017ConBi..31..394H |issn=0888-8892|url-access=subscription }} This is a pressing matter to make sense of as wildlife hunting and trade networks extend all of the provinces of Sulawesi, and is undergoing rapid exploitation. Over 2,100 individuals were reported in trade records from Indonesia in 1991, though no quotas were set in subsequent years. Despite these pressures, its ability to persist in disturbed habitats may buffer it against rapid decline
